hard.next_step: 20% confidence 1st June- Workshop confirmed 9th June- planning agenda 15th May - Workshop targeted for 1st week June 22nd April - Working with Danny for Demo date in the first week of May Presented corporate deck on 7th April to Danny - Head of supply chain & procurement Demo date- To be scheduled for May 2026 as per prospect availability.
hard.ais_why: Early-stage traditional S2C engagement with no AI SKUs in scope (iContract; iSupplier; eProcurement; eInvoicing; iSaaS; iRisk) and no AI-related buyer ask captured in Next Step. Danny Lau said on 7 Apr he has 'no problem using AI' but his AI initiatives are separate and have not reached procurement yet.
hard.competitor: Unnamed supplier-portal product vendor; status quo (manual procurement)

north_star_verdict.headline: Cold for ~90 days: Danny Lau engaged well on the 7 Apr intro but has not replied to any Zycus outreach since his 10 Apr email; the promised demo/workshop never happened and the deal never left Qualified.
north_star_verdict.math: Today 6 Jul 2026. Days to close: 116 (close 30 Oct 2026). Stage: Qualified (early), qualified 30 Apr 2026. Time-in-stage: ~67 days in Qualified, ~90 days since creation (7 Apr). Last GENUINE two-way buyer exchange: 10 Apr 2026 email ('I will revert by next week') and the 7 Apr discovery call, ~87-90 days ago. Every touch since (5 May follow-up call, 8 May summit invites to six contacts, 15 May demo follow-up, 13 Jun Clari event invite) is rep-side outbound with NO buyer reply. LastActivityDate 13 Jun is a rep Clari email, not buyer engagement. Buyer dark 60+ days forces Off Track. Remaining path (detailed demo, multi-stakeholder eval, ROI validation, pricing, EB sign-off, MSA/SOW, InfoSec) has not even started; the 30 Oct date is not credible.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Pipeline (correct) - the deal is genuinely early-stage and has gone dark; Pipeline matches reality. The close date, not the category, is the problem: 30 Oct is not achievable and should move to 2027 once the buyer re-engages.
evidence: 7 Apr 2026: Rich discovery call with Danny Lau Kok Cheng (Head, Supply Chain; Decision Maker contact role). Danny confirmed procurement is fully manual despite a live Oracle ERP, articulated resource-waste pain, and stated an end-to-end source-to-pay preference. Source: Avoma transcript 7 Apr 2026.
10 Apr 2026: Danny replied to demo-scheduling email 'I will revert by next week.' This is the LAST inbound buyer touch on record. Source: SF Task/Email 10 Apr 2026.

8 May 2026: Zycus sent Agentic AI Procurement Summit invites to all six contacts (Danny, Ai Chew Ley, Terrance Chai, Jonathan Goh, Hwan Lim, Koon Ming); no reply logged from any. Source: SF Tasks 8 May 2026.
5 May and 15 May 2026: Outbound follow-up call and demo-scheduling attempt to Danny, both unanswered. Source: SF Tasks 5 May / 15 May 2026.
13 Jun 2026: [Clari] email invited Danny to a 21/22 Jul Singapore procurement event; no reply. This is the most recent activity and it is rep-side, not buyer engagement. Source: SF Task 13 Jun 2026.

Next_Step__c claims a workshop 'confirmed 9th June' with 'planning agenda', but NO Salesforce activity, event, or Avoma call corroborates that a June workshop or any post-April demo actually occurred - the field is aspirational, not evidence of engagement. Source: Next_Step__c vs activity trail.

No Economic Buyer engaged. Danny defers to 'the bosses/managers' for the ~RM1.2M spend ('I don't have a budget in place, but if the need is there I can get it done'). Koon Ming (Executive Vice Chairman) is on the contact list as the likely budget authority but has never been engaged. Source: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026; OpportunityContactRole.
AIS is Negative (2.0): no AI SKUs in scope and no AI buying signal, despite the rep pitching Merlin/agentic heavily on 7 Apr. Source: AIS_Score__c / AIS_Status__c.

metrics.narrative: Danny Lau articulated clear qualitative pain on the 7 Apr call: procurement is 'still a very manual process' despite a live Oracle ERP, and 'we are wasting a lot of resources where it should not be. We should be looking in the market trying to get the best price, but instead we are working on a lot of these manual tasks that I think can be simplified.' No savings figure was quantified BY the buyer; the only number is rep-asserted (Josh claimed 3-5% savings on Press Metal's 500M+ annual spend). Danny himself set the bar: 'The bosses will ask, we're gonna spend 1,200,000, where's the value... if I can't get that number for them, what's the point?' So the metric that matters (proven savings) is stated as the buying gate but not yet built. Quantifying it is the move.
sources: Avoma call 7 Apr 2026, Zycus rep (Josh) on 3-5% savings

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer is engaged and none is named in a MEDDPICC field. Danny Lau (DM) controls procurement but explicitly does NOT hold budget: 'I don't have a budget in place, but if the need is there, I can get it done' and 'if the managers all agree, it's done on the spot' - so the true budget authority is the family-business leadership ('the bosses'). The only executive on the Salesforce contact list who plausibly fits is Koon Ming, Executive Vice Chairman (koon.ming@pressmetalna.com), but he is tagged only as Evaluator and has never appeared on a call, email reply, or activity. The evidence shows a fast, ROI-gated approval chain but no identified, engaged budget owner. The move is to get Danny to name and route Zycus to whoever signs off the ~RM1.2M spend.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Koon Ming, Executive Vice Chairman (Evaluator)

decision_criteria.narrative: Danny named several real criteria on 7 Apr even though no formal RFP or scored criteria list exists. He wants: end-to-end source-to-pay ('End to end because I cannot answer for finance... but if there's value in it, why not?'), a supplier portal where suppliers input fluctuating prices themselves ('I also want the supplier to use the portal to input their prices because we buy a lot of things that fluctuate day to day'), local/SME supplier onboarding (many local suppliers lack systems), and ESG/sustainability supplier compliance ('we also have compliance sustainability... sustainable ESG procurement'). Above all sits the overriding criterion: provable ROI/savings. These are buyer-stated but not yet validated against a demo, so they are confirmed as wants and inferred as the eventual scoring basis.

decision_process.narrative: Read from the call sequence, the process barely started: a 30 Mar intro Danny no-showed, then the real 7 Apr introductory call, after which the buyer went silent. Danny described a fast, informal approval process typical of a family-run business: 'the approval process is very fast... if the managers all agree, it's done on the spot. The chain of command is very fast.' The gating step is a value/ROI demonstration to 'the bosses', not a procurement committee. No formal evaluation stages, no timeline in writing, and no next event has been booked - the process is stalled at 'buyer agreed to schedule a demo' with the demo never scheduled. Getting the evaluation steps and a decision date in writing is required to rebuild the process map.
sources: Avoma calls 30 Mar and 7 Apr 2026, Danny Lau

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics are in view - expected at Qualified stage with no demo yet delivered. As a new-business full-suite S2C/S2P deal this would eventually need an MSA, an Order Form and a SOW (SOW signed separately by the AVP Global Delivery and typically the signature predictor), plus InfoSec review given Jonathan Goh (Head of IT) is a listed evaluator and Oracle ERP integration is in scope. Malaysia/APAC would normally issue a PO to gate invoicing. None of this is live; there is no compelling event or contract-expiration forcing function on record. Visibility only comes after the demo and ROI case restart the deal - too early to be a risk today.
sources: OpportunityContactRole: Jonathan Goh, Head of Information Technology

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is clear, owned, and confirmed by the pain owner himself. Danny Lau, who heads the whole supply chain (logistics plus procurement), wants to lift his team from tactical purchasing to strategic procurement: 'I want to elevate the team to procurement so they do more strategic decisions, long-term planning, because I don't want to spend time on day to day.' The concrete pain is manual quotation gathering, PO creation, and invoice matching consuming his team, plus multi-plant sourcing complexity (East vs West Malaysia pricing and lead-time trade-offs). He also flagged a supplier-side gap: he wants suppliers to self-input fluctuating prices via a portal, and wants ESG/sustainability supplier compliance. Pain is genuine and articulated; what is missing is the quantified cost of that pain.

champion.narrative: Danny Lau Kok Cheng (Head, Supply Chain; Decision Maker contact role) is a developing champion, not yet a strong one. What developed: he owns the pain, drove the discovery conversation, stated urgency ('if I can have it fast, I'll prefer this quarter'), asked for the deck to share internally, and verbally agreed to book a detailed demo/workshop. What is missing and now regressing: he does not control budget, has not introduced Zycus to any executive or finance stakeholder, is evaluating at least one rival supplier-portal vendor, and - critically - has gone silent for ~90 days, not replying to any of five-plus outreach touches since his 10 Apr 'I will revert next week'. A champion who stops responding is a champion at risk. Re-securing his engagement and converting his verbal interest into a booked demo is the prerequisite to every other step.

competition.narrative: The dominant competitor on this deal today is inertia / do-nothing. Danny rejected staying manual in words, but with no budget allocated, no demo booked, other AI initiatives running 'in the background', and 90 days of silence, the most likely outcome is that Press Metal simply keeps managing procurement manually and never buys - a no-decision risk, not a loss to a named rival. The one real rival is an unnamed supplier-portal PRODUCT vendor Danny said was visiting in mid-April ('I am also looking at one other supplier portal by next week or so; they propose something quite interesting'); its name, scope, and status are unknown. Zycus's wedge is breadth (full end-to-end source-to-pay vs a point portal) plus local proof (15 Malaysia customers, 80% local suppliers, SME/handwritten-invoice onboarding). We must discover the rival's name and, more urgently, break the buyer's silence.

deal_movement.summary: The deal moved forward once (30 Apr Initial Interest -> Qualified) then went dark. After Danny's 10 Apr 'I will revert next week', every touch is rep-side and unanswered; the Next_Step 'workshop confirmed 9 June' is not corroborated by any activity. Real pulse: cold since mid-April.

items.change: Last genuine inbound buyer touch: Danny Lau emailed 'I will revert by next week' about demo scheduling
Stage advanced Initial Interest -> Qualified (George John)
Close date pushed 30 Sep -> 30 Oct 2026 (George John), a rep-side date move with no buyer trigger
Six Agentic AI Summit invites sent to all contacts; no buyer reply
Demo-scheduling follow-up attempt to Danny; unanswered
Clari email inviting Danny to 21/22 Jul Singapore procurement event; unanswered (latest activity, rep-side)

competitive_position.summary: The single strongest CURRENT threat is inertia / do-nothing: Press Metal has no allocated budget, has gone dark for ~90 days, and runs other AI initiatives in the background, so keeping procurement manual and never deciding is the likeliest loss path (a no-decision, not a competitor loss). Second is an unnamed supplier-portal product vendor Danny was reviewing in mid-April (scope narrower than Zycus's full source-to-pay; name and status still unknown). Zycus differentiates on end-to-end breadth and strong local Malaysia proof, but competitive strategy is blind until we learn the rival's name and, first, break the silence.

competitors.name: Do nothing / status quo (manual procurement)
competitors.quote: In terms of procurement we are still, to me, it's still a very manual process... we are wasting a lot of resources where it should not be.
competitors.how_we_win: Danny rejects manual in principle, but with no budget and 90 days of silence, inertia is winning. Break it by quantifying the cost of doing nothing (3-5% of 500M+ spend) and putting a low-friction phased demo in front of Danny to restart momentum.

competitors.name: Unnamed supplier-portal product vendor
competitors.quote: I am also looking at one other, what they call it, supplier portal by next week or so. They propose something that's quite interesting, the idea.
competitors.how_we_win: This is a point supplier-portal product, narrower than Zycus's end-to-end source-to-pay. Differentiate on breadth (sourcing, contracts, eProcurement, eInvoicing, supplier risk) and local proof (15 Malaysia customers, SME/handwritten-invoice onboarding). Urgent: get Danny to name it and its status.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Zycus fits Danny's stated wants well on paper (end-to-end automation, supplier portal, local/SME onboarding, ESG compliance) but the decisive expectation - provable ROI/savings - is unmet, and no demo has validated any of it.

best_practice_check.summary: The deal is single-threaded on a champion who has gone dark, has no engaged budget owner, and no AI resonance despite a Merlin-heavy pitch. These three levers decide whether it revives.
flags: Re-open the single live thread: Danny Lau has not replied in ~90 days - George John must re-engage him directly before the deal dies, then multi-thread to Jonathan Goh (IT) and Terrance Chai.
No economic buyer is engaged - the family-business bosses / Koon Ming (Executive Vice Chairman) gate the ~RM1.2M; get Danny to name and route Zycus to the actual budget owner.

AIS is Negative with no AI SKUs and a stalled Merlin pitch - either drop the agentic framing or tie procurement-AI directly to Danny's stated 'elevate my team to strategic work' goal, and lead with quantified savings, not AI.

champion_strength.summary: Danny Lau Kok Cheng (Head, Supply Chain; Decision Maker) is a developing champion who is now at risk. He owned the pain, drove the 7 Apr discovery, asked for the deck internally, and verbally agreed to a demo - but he does not control budget, has opened no access to power, is weighing a rival portal, and has gone silent for ~90 days. What developed: pain ownership and stated urgency. What is missing: budget authority, an executive introduction, and, right now, any response at all. Re-securing him is the prerequisite to reviving the deal.
champion_strength.champion: Danny Lau Kok Cheng (Head, Supply Chain)

ai_positioning_strength.summary: The rep pitched Merlin/agentic AI heavily on 7 Apr (121 agents, 40% task reduction), but it did not land: Danny said his AI initiatives are separate and 'have not reached procurement yet', and AIS reads Negative. AI was delivered but is not a buying driver here - savings is. Lead with ROI, not AI.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS field is Negative (2.0): no AI SKUs in scope (iContract; iSupplier; eProcurement; eInvoicing; iSaaS; iRisk) and no AI-related buyer ask. Calls corroborate: Danny said 'I have no problem using AI' but his AI initiatives are in the background and not yet in procurement, so the appetite is polite rather than active. The agentic pitch did not convert into a stated AI requirement.

No economic buyer engaged; the ~RM1.2M spend hinges on unengaged family-business leadership / Koon Ming.

Single-threaded on Danny; IT, finance, and the exec sponsor are all unengaged, so a silent champion means a silent deal.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement has cooled to cold: strong single discovery call on 7 Apr, then zero buyer response to five-plus rep touches over ~90 days. The deal is dark, not merely slowing.

Six 8 May summit invites and 5/15 May demo attempts all unanswered.
Latest activity (13 Jun Clari invite) is rep-side with no response.

items.trigger: Danny said 'I will revert by next week' on 10 Apr and has not replied to any outreach since.
items.expected_effect: Breaks a 90-day silence and tests whether the deal is still alive before writing it off.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A promising early S2C discovery that has gone cold: Danny Lau (Head of Supply Chain) engaged well on the 7 Apr intro but has not replied to any Zycus outreach in ~90 days, no demo was ever delivered, no budget is allocated, no economic buyer is engaged, and a rival supplier-portal vendor is in the mix.

win_position.text: This is a weak, early-stage position: the deal never left Qualified, no demo has been delivered, and the champion has stopped responding for about 90 days, so there is no proven value, no engaged buyer, and no momentum to build on.
The upside is a real, owned pain: Danny Lau said manual procurement is 'wasting a lot of resources' and he wants to elevate his team to strategic work (Avoma, 7 Apr).
No economic buyer is engaged - Danny said 'I don't have a budget in place' and the ~RM1.2M decision sits with the family-business bosses / Koon Ming, who Zycus has never met (Avoma, 7 Apr).
A competitor is live and blind: Danny was reviewing an unnamed supplier-portal product vendor in mid-April and we still do not know its name or status (Avoma, 7 Apr).

deal_momentum.text: Momentum has collapsed: every touch since Danny's 10 Apr 'I will revert next week' - the 8 May summit invites, 15 May demo attempt, 13 Jun Clari invite - went unanswered.
The Next_Step claim of a '9 June workshop confirmed' is contradicted by the activity trail, which shows no June buyer engagement at all.

customer_commitment.text: The only commitment was verbal and unfulfilled: Danny agreed to schedule a demo on 7 Apr and then went silent, giving no date and no team availability.
Commitment is gated on savings Danny has not seen: 'if I can't get that number for them, what's the point?' (Avoma, 7 Apr).

deal_risk.text: The primary risk is a no-decision: no budget, 90 days dark, and background AI initiatives make 'keep doing procurement manually' the likeliest outcome, not a loss to a rival.
Single-threading amplifies it - IT (Jonathan Goh), finance, and the exec sponsor are all unengaged, so one silent champion means the whole deal is silent.

champion_strength.evidence: Danny is a developing champion gone dark for ~90 days with no access to power (Avoma 7 Apr; no reply since 10 Apr).

customer_preference.evidence: No Zycus-specific lean; evaluating a rival portal and unresponsive.

deal_momentum.evidence: Zero buyer response to 5+ outreach touches over 90 days.

scope_change.detail: Scope is the S2C/S2P module set (iContract; iSupplier; eProcurement; eInvoicing; iSaaS; iRisk) at 150k; no narrowing versus the prior record or the original scoping.

critical_signals.text: Danny Lau engaged strongly on the 7 April intro but has gone silent for about 90 days and the demo he agreed to was never booked.
There is no allocated budget - Danny said the roughly RM1.2 million spend only happens if Zycus proves the savings the bosses will demand.
A rival supplier-portal product vendor was reviewing Press Metal in mid-April and its name and status are still unknown.
Koon Ming, Executive Vice Chairman and the likely budget authority, sits on the account but has never been engaged.

customer_preference.evidence: Danny was polite and interested in discovery but asked only for a demo, is weighing a rival supplier portal, and has since gone silent - no Zycus-specific lean.

business_case.evidence: Qualitative pain (manual waste, resource drain) is clear; the 3-5% savings figure is rep-asserted, not buyer-confirmed or quantified against Press Metal's actual spend.

calls_omitted.meeting: Introductory Meeting with Press Metal & Zycus (30 Mar 2026)
calls_omitted.reason: Buyer no-showed; internal rep-only wait/discussion, no buyer content.

gaps: Only one substantive buyer call (7 Apr); the 30 Mar meeting was a no-show, so evidence density is thin and single-threaded.
Economic buyer / budget owner never engaged; Koon Ming (Exec Vice Chairman) is the inferred authority from Salesforce but unconfirmed.
No formal evaluation criteria, process, or decision date in writing.
